title: द्वितीयोनुवाकः
pada: नमः॑ । उ॒च्चैः-घो॑षाय । आ॒-क्र॒न्दय॑ते । प॒त्ती॒नाम् । पत॑ये । नमो॒ ॥
krama: नम॑ उ॒च्चैर्घो॑षाय । उ॒च्चैर्घो॑षायाक्र॒न्दय॑ते । आ॒क्र॒न्दय॑ते पत्ती॒नाम् । पत्ती॒ना-म्पत॑ये । पत॑ये॒ नमो॒ । नमो॒ इति॑ नमो॒ ॥

sayana.text:
'Uchairghoshah' is he whose sound (ghosha) or noise is loud/raised (uchrita) during war. 'Akrandayate'—to him who makes enemies wail/cry (rodayita). Salutation be to that Rudra. Salutation to the protector of 'pattis'—the warriors who walk on foot (foot-soldiers) are called 'pattis'. (12)

Bhatta-Bhaskara-Bhashya: 'Namah uchairghoshaya'—to him with a loud sound or loud hymns of praise. Or to him in the form of loud sounds like clouds, etc. 'Akrandayate'—to him who causes 'akrandana'—the loud shouting/calling in war. Or to him who makes enemies cry everywhere. It means the one who brings victory in wars. 'Pattinam pataye namah':
"One chariot, one elephant, five foot-soldiers, and three horses—these together are called a 'patti'."
To their Master. Or: to the Lord of foot-soldiers; to him who is capable of conquering all three worlds himself in the form of a foot-soldier. (12)

He speaks the thirteenth Yajus:
Salutation to the one who surrounds the whole (army), to the one who runs, salutation to the lord of the good/attendants.

abhinava_shankara.text:
---
84                    Sri Rudra Bhashyam
---

Here the word "suvantaya" expresses the reason for His lordship over herbs: since the Supreme Lord is the master of the three worlds consisting of the entire earth-sphere, His lordship over herbs — which are transformations of the earth — also follows from that same [fact]. By the word "varivaskritaya," expressing His dwelling in the body of devotees, the being-eaten-by-the-Lord aspect is revealed — as the Lord Himself says through the mouth of [scripture], conveying His role as the enjoyer: "I am not pleased in the same way by fire-sacrifice, by the oblation offered by the sacrificer at the time of revelation, as I am by [the worship of] one who moves about freely and joyfully, satisfied [with simple service], marked with the signs of his own deeds." By that [status as] enjoyer of the fruit of herbs, the Supreme Lord's lordship over herbs is indicated — on the principle that it is appropriate that the master of something be the enjoyer of its fruit. Thus it is stated. Contemplating precisely this exalted quality of being food-for-the-Lord, [scripture] declares — through Shruti, Smriti, and other sources — that food prepared by devotees is indeed food prepared [for Him], and not that prepared by others: "The one who craves inwardly [the food offered] with the heart, the Supreme One grasps with the mind; he grasps the taste with the tongue; the exclusive sinner becomes the exclusive eater" — such Shruti passages; and from the Parashara Purana: "He who always craves [the food offered] inwardly with the heart and with devotion, grasps it with his tongue as taste-filled, immortal water. But those who do not offer [food] inwardly to Bhava together with Bhavani — they grasp only excrement with the tongue, without doubt." Thus ends the eleventh.

[Mantra 12:]
Obeisance to the one of loud cry, to the one who makes enemies weep, to the Lord of foot-soldiers — obeisance.
abhinava_shankara.sanskrit:
---
८४                    श्रीरुद्रभाष्यम् ।
---

ष्वादीनाम्, पितिः स्वामी, तस्मै नमः इत्यर्थः । अत्र
सुवंतय इत्यनेन ओषधिस्वामित्वे हेतुरुच्यते, सकलभूवल-
यत्रयस्थया तत्स्वामिनः परमेश्वरस्य भूविकारौषधिस्वामित्-
वमापि तत एव प्रास्नेः; वारिवस्कृतायेत्यनेन च भक्तकाय-
स्थत्वोक्त्या 'नाहं तथाग्निं यजमानह्विर्विदतानि च्योतनुत-
युतमदनहुतसुखुखेन। यद्राक्षणस्य सुखतश्वरतोऽनुघासं तुष्टस्य
मत्यविह्वैर्निजकमेणांकैः' इति भगवतोक्तं सत्मुखेन भग-
वतो भोक्तृत्वमाविष्कृतम् । तेन चौषधिफलभोक्तृत्वेनापि
परमेश्वरस्यौषधिस्वामित्वं  तत्फलभोक्तुस्तस्वासित्वौचित्यात्
इत्युक्तं भवति । एवं परमेश्वरभोजनरूपत्वातिशयमभिप्रेत्येव
उपासकैः कृतमेव भे भोजनं नान्ये: कृतमिति श्रुतिस्मृत्यादि-
भिराज्ञायते— 'अन्तरिच्छति तं जनो हृदं परमो मनीषया
गृह्णाति जिह्वया रसं केवलाघो भवति केवलादी' इत्याधा-
श्रुतयः, 'अन्तरिच्छति यो हृदं सदा वन्दं मनीष्या। गृह्णाति
जिह्वया सोऽस्नं रसपूर्णामृतोद्कम् ।  अन्तरनेच्छिन्ते ये हृदं
भवान्या सहितं भवम् ।  पुरीषमेव गृह्णन्ति जिह्वया ते न
संशयः' इत्यादिपराशरपुराणवचनानि । इत्येकादशः ॥

नम उच्चैर्घोषायाक्रन्दयते पत्तीनां प-
तये नमः ॥
